title: mentha arvensis subsp. austriaca
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. austriaca, also known as wild mint or field mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that grows in the wild and is native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Mentha arvensis subsp. austriaca include its ability to act as an antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative, making it useful for treating digestive issues and relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce nausea, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its ability to help lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. Mentha arvensis subsp. austriaca is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or used topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
